data.title: test-batched.sh exits with code 144 despite being designed to avoid timeouts
data.description: test-batched.sh is specifically designed to exit before the ~73s tool timeout ceiling by tracking elapsed time and yielding control. However, during sprint execution of epic 0d66-ceb6, multiple consecutive invocations of test-batched.sh (with state file resume) exited with code 144 (SIGURG). This suggests either: (1) the internal time tracking underestimates actual elapsed time, (2) individual test files exceed the per-test budget without the wrapper detecting it, or (3) the 50s timeout parameter is too close to the ~73s ceiling when combined with startup overhead. Reproduction: run test-batched.sh --timeout=50 --runner=bash --test-dir=tests/scripts in a worktree with 227+ test files. Observe exit 144 on multiple resume cycles.
